articles.title: Brent Crude Dips Below $80

articles.title: Oil Prices Slip Amid Strait of Hormuz Reopening Prospects
articles.description: Oil prices have dropped to three-month lows influenced by the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, weaker physical demand, and a preliminary Iran war ceasefire agreement lacking full details. Analysts warn of volatility risks, as negotiations on the deal progress and as China’s crude imports decline.

articles.title: Iran-US Peace Deal Lowers Oil Prices, But India’s Energy Security Risks Remain High
articles.description: Explore how the Iran-US peace deal and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz are easing global oil and LNG prices, yet leaving India’s energy worries unresolved due to high import dependence, elevated shipping costs and persistent geopolitical risks.

articles.title: Global Market | European shares inch up as investors assess US-Iran agreement
articles.description: European stock exchanges kicked off the day with a bullish trend on Tuesday, riding the wave of Monday's upward momentum. Market participants are closely tracking a preliminary deal between the U.S. and Iran, which could initiate oil flow through the vital Strait of Hormuz once again. With oil prices potentially on the decline, inflationary pressures seem to be lessening.

articles.description: Government says E20 fuel use will not void motor insurance, calls viral rejection claims fake, expands ethanol blending and exempts E22 to E30 petrol from central excise duty

articles.title: US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longer
articles.description: The US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

articles.title: Petrol, Ethanol Or Flex Fuel? Your Fuel Choice Could Save You Lakhs
